titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to Elizabeth Tyrrell 15, April, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: References the Battle of Vimy Ridge.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
14 15 April 1917 My dear Mamy I have just received Minnies long
letter of 9th which I was delighted to get. I
had not got any fo since Thursday until
today. The weather here is alternately
bad and good. today happens to be
very wet. There is not much to be
done today except write letters. We
were at church at ten and have
nothing to do for the rest of the day. I went into a big town yesterday
afternoon just to look round. Have
some tea and come back again. There was a cinema show on Friday
night but the films were not up to
much and some bright fellow started
making a row like animals and in
a minute every one in the room
was trying to imitate animals. I The finish was one man imitating
a pig's last moments. The C.O, who
was there was highly amused. There is certainly great war
news now. Some fools are talking
about the hum arranging the retreats etc. t it is not a a cass of what he
will do but a case who of what
he has jolly well got to do or
be reminded by good sharp steel. There is no doubt that he is
being very hard pressed. I see he
says he retired according to plan from
Vinny. some plan !!! He did not say
who made the plan. I was very sorry to see in the
casualty list about Four Taving. I
saw it the day I got Daddy's
letter telling of it.
